questions.q: A certain saturated hydrocarbon effuses about half as fast as $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ vapour. Number of carbons in the formula of this hydrocarbon are?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A mercury column of length 10 cm is in the middle of a horizontal tube of length 1 m closed at both ends containing same amount of a gas in both columns. If the tube is placed vertically, the mercury column will shift through a distance of $\frac{225}{13} \mathrm{~cm}$ from its initial position. Find the final pressure of gas in upper column (in cm of Hg ) when the tube is placed vertically.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A flask containing same He gas at $1.5 \mathrm{~atm} \& 300 \mathrm{~K}$ is connected to another flask, four times larger in volume containing $\mathrm{N}_2$ at same temperature and pressure by means of narrow negligible volume tube. The bigger flask was then kept in a thermostat both maintained at 500 K , while other still at 300 K . Find final pressure. (mark the answer in atm)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{n}_1=\mathrm{x} \text { moles } \quad \mathrm{n}_2=4 \mathrm{x} \text { moles } \\ & \frac{V_1}{\mathrm{n}_1 T_1}=\frac{V_2}{n_2 T_2}\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{V}{n_1 \times 300}=\frac{4 V}{n_2 \times 500}...(i)$ And $\mathrm{n}_2+\mathrm{n}_1=\mathrm{x}+4 \mathrm{x}=5 \mathrm{x}...(ii)$ From (i) \& (ii) $n_1=\frac{25 x}{17}$ Then $\frac{P_1}{P_2}=\frac{n_1}{n_2}$ in flask 1. $\frac{1.5}{P}=\frac{x}{n_1} \Rightarrow P=2.2 \mathrm{~atm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
